Exhibitors.Company Description: 2-3 sentence description

3DEO, Inc.™ designs and builds the world's most capable commercially available airborne lidar systems. Our Geiger-mode lidar sensors leverage patent-pending agile geo-referenced multi-axis scanning to create highly accurate 3D point clouds for infrastructure survey and environmental issues. Built through decades of Department of Defense investment, this method of aerial surveying provides mapping solutions with unmatched collection rates.

Balko designs a modular geospatial data sensor for drones. With swappable LiDAR, cameras, and INS modules on a computer core system, it offers unmatched flexibility. It can adapt to diverse projects, upgrading with new technology, and empowering professionals to capture precise data efficiently in their surveying, mapping, or inspection missions.

The Canadian Remote Sensing Society—Société canadienne de télédétection (CRSS-SCT) is a fully independent, not-for-profit professional society that provides a focal point for leadership and excellence to advance the remote sensing field. The CRSS-SCT maintains partnerships with other organizations in remote sensing, across Canada and abroad.

C-CORE is a Canadian research and development organization with world-leading capability in remote sensing, specializing in satellite-based earth observation, geospatial analysis and deep learning techniques. C-CORE has over 50 years of experience in the advancement of technologies for environmental and industrial monitoring, maritime surveillance and natural resource management.

CERFO advances technological innovation in the forest sector through AI-powered, multi-platform and multi-sensor remote sensing. It delivers end-to-end, optimized data workflows—from field acquisition to decision-ready outputs—and enables partners to independently integrate advanced technologies across forest, agroforestry, and urban ecosystems.
Exhibitors.Company Name: Centre d'enseignement et de recherche en foresterie (CERFO)

Exhibitors.Company Description: Elsevier partners with the global scientific community to advance knowledge through a trusted portfolio of journals, books, and open access titles. We proudly publish the ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing and the ISPRS Open Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing, supporting discovery and collaboration in geospatial and remote sensing research.
Exhibitors.Website:
ISPRS Open Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing: https://www.sciencedirect.com/journal/isprs-open-journal-of-photogrammetry-and-remote-sensing

ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing: https://www.sciencedirect.com/journal/isprs-journal-of-photogrammetry-and-remote-sensing

Exhibitors.Company Description: GeoCue provides drone, mobile, and handheld surveying equipment, point cloud software, workflows, training, and support for high-accuracy LiDAR and imagery 3D mapping. With TrueView sensors and LP360 software, we lead LiDAR processing in North America, meeting professionals’ needs across technology, adoption, budget, and resources for successful geospatial projects.

GEOSYS Alliance delivers comprehensive geospatial solutions, including high-precision imagery, LiDAR, GIS mapping, and remote sensing. Drawing on the combined expertise of GEOSYS Technology Solutions and its subsidiaries, Geodesy Group and The Airborne Sensing Corporation, we provide accurate, timely geospatial intelligence to support informed decision-making and actionable insights across multiple industries.

With more than 200 years of history, Leica Geosystems, part of Hexagon, is the trusted supplier of premium sensors, software and services. Delivering value every day to professionals in surveying, construction, infrastructure, mining, mapping and other geospatial content-dependent industries, Leica Geosystems leads the industry with innovative solutions to empower our autonomous future.

LSGI is a leading department in Geomatics, offering undergraduate and postgraduate programmes in land surveying, geo-informatics, and utility management. With advanced laboratories and expert faculty, LSGI focuses on spatial data science, smart cities, and environmental applications, preparing graduates for professional careers in Hong Kong and globally.
Exhibitors.Company Name: Hong Kong Polytechnic University, Department of Land Surveying & Geo-Informatics

Exhibitors.Company Description: HySpex is Norsk Elektro Optikk’s line of high-performance hyperspectral cameras. Based in Norway, NEO has been a leader in electro-optics R&D since its foundation in 1985. HySpex delivers versatile solutions from ground-based and airborne platforms to space, serving academic research, environmental monitoring, industrial, and defense applications worldwide.

The Institute for Geospatial Inquiry, Instruction and Innovation (i4Geo) at the University of Lethbridge is a collaborative research and training hub connecting academia, industry, and government. i4Geo advances geospatial science through applied research, hands-on workshops, and capacity building in GIS, remote sensing, and spatial data science.

The Geoscience and Remote Sensing Society (GRSS), part of IEEE, advances geoscience and remote sensing through research, engineering, applications, and education. With over 8,000 members across 102+ countries, it unites scientists and engineers, fostering interdisciplinary collaboration, technological innovation, knowledge exchange, and professional engagement from students to life members diverse worldwide.
Exhibitors.Company Name: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Society (GRSS)

Exhibitors.Company Description: The International Society for Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing (ISPRS) is a non-governmental organization that promotes international cooperation between the worldwide organizations with interests in the photogrammetric, remote sensing and spatial information sciences. Established in 1910, the ISPRS is the oldest international professional umbrella organization in its field.

ISPRS Student Consortium is the official representation of the youth to ISPRS. Its main purpose is to link students, young researchers and professionals worldwide, interested in photogrammetry, remote sensing and spatial information science. It acts as a platform to organise events that integrate students and the youth more effectively into ISPRS activities.

ITRES is a global leader in manufacturing hyperspectral and thermal sensors and providing aerial mapping services. Our sensors span the visible through longwave infrared spectrum, delivering advanced imaging analytics for defense, disaster management, object detection, as well as mapping applications in forestry, agriculture, coastal zones, geology, wildfires and search and rescue.

KSGPC is Korea’s leading academic society advancing spatial information science and technology. Through research, academic exchange, and collaboration with industry and government, it promotes innovation, global competitiveness, international cooperation, and professional talent development, contributing to a sustainable spatial information society connecting knowledge, technology, and the world.
Exhibitors.Company Name: Korean Society of Surveying Geodesy Photogrammetry and Cartography

Exhibitors.Company Description: LAStools LiDAR Processing – rapidlasso GmbH, offers a powerful software suite of 54 high-performance, batch-scriptable, multicore tools designed for fast and efficient point cloud processing. Whether you prefer standalone command-line execution, a user-friendly GUI, or toolboxes for QGIS and ArcGIS Pro, LAStools delivers seamless solutions across Windows and Linux platforms.

LIDAR Magazine, a Spatial Media publication, promotes the adoption of lidar and imaging technology. Dr. Stewart Walker, managing editor, oversees an expert team of contributors, bringing insights and commentary to readers via quarterly print editions, newsletters, a podcast program and more. Join us as we map the lidar revolution.

MDPI, established in 1996, is a pioneer in open access publishing, supporting academic communities globally. It has 472 journals, including 463 peer-reviewed ones and 9 conference journals. Among them, the ISPRS International Journal of Geo-Information publishes research articles, reviews, and communications in the field of geoinformation science and its applications.

Moganshan Geospatial Information Laboratory aims to build a world-class, open and hub-type center for spatio-temporal information technology innovation and intelligent applications. It focuses on researching and developing core geospatial intelligence technologies, building a national innovative force for geospatial information technology, and providing high-level scientific support for global governance system construction, digital administration, and digital economy development.

National Cheng Kung University's High Definition Maps Research Center integrates domestic and foreign technology and information to promote the process of HD Maps generation and standard assessment in Taiwan. We promote the cooperation between industry, government and academia to develop the application of HD Maps and autonomous driving.

OroraTech is a global intelligence-as-a-service company that uses high-resolution thermal data from public and proprietary satellites to provide integrated solutions for wildfire detection and monitoring in more than 25 countries. Founded in 2018, OroraTech is headquartered in Munich, Germany, with subsidiaries in Canada, Greece and the United States.

Pix4D is a global leader in photogrammetry software based in Lausanne, Switzerland, with offices in the USA, Germany, Spain, Romania, and Japan. The company develops end-to-end mapping solutions that convert images into survey-grade georeferenced maps, 3D models, and other business-critical insights using advanced algorithms based on computer vision, photogrammetry, and machine learning.

Planet is a leading provider of global, daily satellite imagery and geospatial solutions. Planet is driven by a mission to image the world every day, and make change visible, accessible and actionable. Planet is a public benefit corporation listed on the New York Stock Exchange as PL.

RIEGL, based in Austria, is one of the leading providers of terrestrial, mobile, bathymetric, airborne, and UAV-based laser scanning systems for surveying. Focusing on pulsed time-of-flight laser radar technology in multiple wavelengths, RIEGL has been active in research, development and commercial production of LiDAR systems since 1978 and offers various 3D scanners with a wide array of performance characteristics.

SciAps, Inc. is revolutionizing elemental analysis with cutting-edge handheld instrumentation designed to measure any element, anywhere on the planet—and beyond. Now, with the integration of near-infrared spectroscopy (Vis-NIR), SciAps is pushing boundaries even further—enabling geologists and exploration teams to perform real-time mineral reconnaissance with precision in the field.

The SFPT, a Learned society supported by CNES (French space Agency) and IGN (French Mapping agency), promotes progress in photogrammetry and remote sensing through conferences, expert exchanges, and an open access French scientific journal. Meet three of its member organizations at the ISPRS Congress in Toronto, 5–10 July 2026: Data Terra, CS Group, and TopoAirTech. http://sfpt.fr
Exhibitors.Company Name: SFPT (Société Française de Photogrammétrie et de Télédétéction)
Shaanxi TIRAIN Science & Technology Co., Ltd., 陕西天润科技股份有限公司

Exhibitors.Company Description: SPIE is the international society for optics and photonics. Serving a global membership of over 25,000, we bring engineers, scientists, students, and business professionals together to advance light-based science and technology. SPIE strengthens the global optics and photonics community through conferences, publications, and professional development.

Founded in 1798, Taylor & Francis is an international academic publisher committed to scholarly excellence and integrity.  We proudly publish leading titles in remote sensing and GIS such as Canadian Journal of Remote Sensing (official journal of the CRSS-SCT), International Journal of Digital Earth and Big Earth Data.

Toronto Metropolitan University’s (TMU) Faculty of Engineering and Architectural Science (FEAS) champions interdisciplinary research and practical applications that benefit people and communities. Named top 10 in Canada for mechanical and civil engineering by ShanghaiRanking, FEAS is leading innovative remote sensing projects to monitor emissions, advance digital twin technologies and more.

Trimble is a global technology company that connects the physical and digital worlds, transforming the ways work gets done. With relentless innovation in precise positioning, modeling and data analytics, Trimble enables essential industries including construction, geospatial and transportation. Whether it's helping customers build and maintain infrastructure, design and construct buildings, optimize global supply chains or map the world, Trimble is at the forefront, driving productivity and progress. For more information about Trimble, visit: www.trimble.com.

Founded in 1907, Laval University's Department of Geomatics Sciences is the only one in Canada to offer bachelor's degree programs in French, leading to careers as land surveyors and geomatics engineers, as well as training at the three cycles of higher education in the field of geomatics. It also plays a strategic role in innovation, analysis, and promotion of geospatial data and technologies.

The University of Calgary’s Department of Geomatics Engineering is a world-renowned, multidisciplinary program dedicated to acquiring, modeling, managing, and analyzing geospatial data to address real-world challenges through cutting-edge technologies such as GNSS, remote sensing, GIS, GeoAI, and autonomous systems.
Exhibitors.Company Name: University of Calgary - Department of Geomatics Engineering

Exhibitors.Company Description: As Canada’s oldest engineering faculty, UNB Engineering has over 170 years of innovation and impact. With programs across two campuses, students gain hands-on experience, strong industry connections, and access to world-class research. Our Geodesy & Geomatics (GGE) program, one of only two accredited in Canada, teaches students to measure, map, and model the world through GIS, surveying, and satellite technologies that drive sustainability and navigation.
Exhibitors.Company Name: University of New Brunswick, Faculty of Engineering

Exhibitors.Company Description: Vexcel Imaging offers industry-leading UltraCam aerial cameras and hybrid camera-LiDAR mapping solutions. Outputs include photogrammetric imagery and point clouds, LiDAR point clouds, and other derived products. Vexcel’s fully integrated UltraMap software offers highest automation with efficient data interaction to create photogrammetric point clouds, DSMs, DTMs, orthoimages and 3D data.

WildFireSat is the first government-owned satellite mission specifically designed to monitor all active wildland fires across Canada daily.
La mission GardeFeu constitue la première mission satellitaire gouvernementale spécialement conçue pour une surveillance quotidienne des feux en activité à la grandeur du pays.

Wyvern, an Edmonton-based space data company, is revolutionizing Earth observation with cutting-edge hyperspectral imagery. Their Dragonette constellation delivers the highest-resolution commercial hyperspectral data from space, powering mission-critical applications in defence, wildfire prevention, environmental monitoring, and resource management. By revealing the unseen chemical and material signatures of our planet, Wyvern enables decision-makers to act faster, smarter, and more sustainably: creating a Better Earth, From Space. Learn more at https://www.wyvern.space/
